metadata.title: Assessment Dates
metadata.subtitle: A plain-English guide to the assessment year, taxpayer deadlines, and major equalization events.
metadata.sourceNote: This prototype uses 2026 dated events where a current PAD monthly reminder was available and otherwise carries annual PAD main-calendar patterns forward with 2026 verification clearly marked as pending.

events.title: Assessment Date
events.plainEnglish: The assessment year starts with property generally valued as of January 1.
events.whyItMatters: This date anchors the year before notices, protests, levies, and tax bills follow later.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Annual list-and-value date appears in the PAD main calendar with real and personal property assessment duties.

events.title: Railroad and Public Service Entity Non-Operating Property Reports
events.plainEnglish: Railroads and public service entities report non-operating property to the county assessor.
events.whyItMatters: This is an early-year reporting item that affects specialized assessment records.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Homestead Exemption Eligibility Date
events.plainEnglish: Homestead exemption eligibility is tied to ownership, occupancy, age, disability, and filing status as of the start of the year.
events.whyItMatters: This helps explain why exemption questions often depend on the taxpayer's status on January 1.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Useful when answering eligibility-timing questions before the February filing window opens.

events.title: Mobile Home Court Report
events.plainEnglish: Mobile home court owners, lessees, or managers file a report with the county assessor.
events.whyItMatters: This keeps mobile home records aligned before later assessment and tax-list steps.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Air Carrier and Car Line Tax Rate Set
events.plainEnglish: The Property Tax Administrator sets the tax rate for air carriers and car line companies.
events.whyItMatters: This is a specialized state-assessed property milestone in the broader assessment cycle.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Specialized property reminder; subdued in the admin reference.

events.title: Preliminary Valuation Change Notices for Large Counties
events.plainEnglish: Counties with a population of at least 150,000 provide preliminary valuation change notices to real property owners.
events.whyItMatters: This creates an early review path in larger counties before the statewide June protest window.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited duty; verify county applicability before using in public copy.

events.title: Large-County Property Record Review Meeting Window
events.plainEnglish: Owners in larger counties must be provided an opportunity to meet with the assessor's office to review the property record and assessed value.
events.whyItMatters: This is an early-year review process that may happen before the main June protest period.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited review window; keep distinct from the June valuation protest window.

events.title: Governmental Subdivision Lease Copies Due
events.plainEnglish: Governmental subdivisions provide county assessors copies of leases or descriptions of leased property.
events.whyItMatters: Lease information helps assessors track property use and taxable status questions.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Rent-Restricted Housing Valuation Approach Petition Deadline
events.plainEnglish: The county board of equalization or Tax Commissioner may petition for use of a different approach to value rent-restricted housing projects.
events.whyItMatters: This specialized deadline can affect how rent-restricted housing projects are valued.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Specialized BOE/state process reminder.

events.title: Homestead Training Webinar
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a homestead training webinar for assessor education credit.
events.whyItMatters: Education reminders help the admin view function as a working monthly reference.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder lists this as a 1-hour credit opportunity.

events.title: County Assessor Coffee Talk
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a county assessor Coffee Talk session.
events.whyItMatters: This gives admin users a quick monthly reminder for training and discussion opportunities.
events.sourceLabel: January 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes no credits are given.

events.title: Homestead Exemption Filing Window
events.plainEnglish: Eligible homeowners may file a Nebraska Homestead Exemption application with the county assessor during this window.
events.whyItMatters: For eligible taxpayers, the exemption can reduce the property tax owed after values and levies are applied.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Coordinate with Form 458 processing, prior-year claimant mailings, and late-filing procedures where applicable.

events.title: Permissive Exemption Review Period
events.plainEnglish: The County Board of Equalization reviews permissive exemption applications during this part of the year.
events.whyItMatters: Exemption decisions affect which property remains in the taxable value base.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Useful for tracking BOE meeting notices, application lists, and assessor recommendations.

events.title: Beginning Farmer Exemption Approval or Denial Notices
events.plainEnglish: County assessors issue approval or denial notices to beginning farmer exemption applicants.
events.whyItMatters: This closes an exemption decision loop early in the assessment year.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Form 1027 reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Aircraft Information Report Deadline
events.plainEnglish: Aircraft hangar owners, lessees, or managers file the Aircraft Information Report with the county assessor.
events.whyItMatters: This supports specialized personal property and aircraft record tracking.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Homestead Claim Forms and Address Lists to Assessors
events.plainEnglish: The Tax Commissioner provides county assessors with homestead exemption claim forms and prior-year applicant address lists.
events.whyItMatters: This supports county mailing and processing work for the homestead filing season.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Pairs with the April homestead application mailing duty.

events.title: Large-County Intent to Meet Deadline
events.plainEnglish: In counties with a population of at least 150,000, owners notify the assessor if they intend to meet about the current-year assessed value.
events.whyItMatters: This is a population-limited early review step before the general protest window.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Verify county applicability before surfacing publicly.

events.title: Assessor Certificate Education Records Mailed
events.plainEnglish: The Property Tax Administrator mails certificate holders completed education records and remaining hours needed for recertification.
events.whyItMatters: This gives assessors an early-year credential and continuing education checkpoint.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Education/certification reminder.

events.title: Real Property Tax Sale List Forwarded to PAD
events.plainEnglish: County treasurers forward an electronic copy of the real property subject-to-sale list for statewide publication.
events.whyItMatters: This is a prior-year collection and publication milestone, separate from current valuation work.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Treasurer-facing carryover item included for context.

events.title: Personal Property Webinar
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a personal property webinar for assessor education credit.
events.whyItMatters: Education reminders help the admin view function as a working monthly reference.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder lists this as a 1-hour credit opportunity.

events.title: County Assessor Coffee Talk
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a county assessor Coffee Talk session.
events.whyItMatters: This gives admin users a quick monthly reminder for training and discussion opportunities.
events.sourceLabel: February 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes no credit is given.

events.title: Real Property Assessment Work Completes
events.plainEnglish: County assessment work is completed and certified before the public notice and protest season.
events.whyItMatters: This is a key internal checkpoint before valuation-change notices and protest rights open.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: The main calendar distinguishes counties by population for real property assessment completion and abstract certification.

events.title: Improvements on Leased Land Application
events.plainEnglish: Owners seeking separate taxation of real property improvements on leased land may file Form 402 with the county assessor.
events.whyItMatters: This keeps ownership and assessment treatment clear for improvements on leased land.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Permissive Exemption Recommendations
events.plainEnglish: County assessors make recommendations on permissive exemption applications and prepare the application list.
events.whyItMatters: This prepares the County Board of Equalization for exemption decisions.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes Form 451 applications and Form 451A reaffirmations.

events.title: Aircraft Information Reports Forwarded to PAD
events.plainEnglish: County assessors forward an electronic copy of aircraft information reports filed with the county to PAD.
events.whyItMatters: This completes the aircraft reporting handoff after the February filing deadline.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder lists pat.tech@nebraska.gov for the electronic copy.

events.title: Improvements on Leased Public Land Application
events.plainEnglish: Owners of improvements on leased public land may file Form 402P with the county assessor.
events.whyItMatters: This supports separate assessment treatment when improvements on public land change ownership.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Notice of Intent to Tax Non-Public-Use Property
events.plainEnglish: County assessors notify governmental subdivisions of intent to tax property not used for a public purpose and not paying in lieu of tax.
events.whyItMatters: This starts a review path for publicly owned property that may not qualify for exemption.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Connects to April protest and May BOE decision reminders.

events.title: Agricultural Market Influence Certification
events.plainEnglish: County assessors certify whether agricultural and horticultural land markets have influences outside the typical market.
events.whyItMatters: This supports special valuation and agricultural land assessment review.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder also references special valuation information when applied.

events.title: TIF Report Submitted to Legislature
events.plainEnglish: The Property Tax Administrator submits a report of active tax increment financing projects to the Legislature.
events.whyItMatters: This is a statewide reporting milestone that helps frame TIF activity.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Subdued statewide context item.

events.title: Small-County Abstract Extension Request Deadline
events.plainEnglish: Counties under the population threshold file any written request for more time to file the County Abstract of Assessment for Real Property.
events.whyItMatters: This is an internal timing checkpoint before abstract certification.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Small-County Six-Year Inspection Review Checkpoint
events.plainEnglish: Smaller counties must inspect and review enough real property parcels to keep all parcels on the six-year review cycle.
events.whyItMatters: This is an assessment-quality and record-maintenance checkpoint.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Small-County Real Property Assessment Completed
events.plainEnglish: Smaller counties complete real property assessment work.
events.whyItMatters: This is a major assessment-roll milestone before notices, equalization, and protests.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Small-County Real Property Abstract Certified
events.plainEnglish: Smaller counties certify the County Abstract of Assessment for Real Property with the Property Tax Administrator.
events.whyItMatters: The abstract is a key filing in the state equalization and assessment review process.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Large-County Abstract Extension Request Deadline
events.plainEnglish: Counties at or above the population threshold file any written request for more time to file the County Abstract of Assessment for Real Property.
events.whyItMatters: This is an internal timing checkpoint before large-county abstract certification.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Large-County Six-Year Inspection Review Checkpoint
events.plainEnglish: Larger counties must inspect and review enough real property parcels to keep all parcels on the six-year review cycle.
events.whyItMatters: This is an assessment-quality and record-maintenance checkpoint.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Large-County Real Property Assessment Completed
events.plainEnglish: Larger counties complete real property assessment work.
events.whyItMatters: This is a major assessment-roll milestone before notices, equalization, and protests.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Large-County Real Property Abstract Certified
events.plainEnglish: Larger counties certify the County Abstract of Assessment for Real Property with the Property Tax Administrator.
events.whyItMatters: The abstract is a key filing in the state equalization and assessment review process.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited deadline; verify county applicability.

events.title: Assessor Comments for Reports and Opinions
events.plainEnglish: County assessors may submit written comments that become part of the Reports and Opinions.
events.whyItMatters: This gives assessors a formal record opportunity before state equalization materials are finalized.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder cites regulatory authority in the source PDF.

events.title: County Assessor Coffee Talk
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a county assessor Coffee Talk session.
events.whyItMatters: This gives admin users a quick monthly reminder for training and discussion opportunities.
events.sourceLabel: March 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes no credits are offered.

events.title: Homestead Application Mailing
events.plainEnglish: Returning homestead exemption applicants should watch for application materials from the county assessor.
events.whyItMatters: Receiving the form is a prompt to review eligibility and avoid missing the filing window.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: The PAD main calendar describes mailing duties for owners granted an exemption in the preceding year.

events.title: Governmental Subdivision Protest Deadline
events.plainEnglish: A state entity, governmental subdivision, or lessee may protest the assessor's determination that property is not being used for a public purpose.
events.whyItMatters: This is a public-use and exemption review checkpoint before County Board decisions.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Connects to the March assessor notice and May BOE decision reminders.

events.title: Disabled or Blind Veteran Mobile Home Exemption Filing
events.plainEnglish: Disabled or blind veterans file the mobile home exemption application by this date.
events.whyItMatters: This is a specific exemption filing deadline separate from the general homestead filing window.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder references Form 453.

events.title: Prior-Year Taxes Delinquent in Larger Counties
events.plainEnglish: In the listed larger counties, first-half prior-year real and personal property taxes become delinquent if unpaid.
events.whyItMatters: This is a payment-status item for the prior tax year, not the current assessment value.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder names Douglas, Lancaster, and Sarpy Counties.

events.title: Reports and Opinions Delivered for Smaller Counties
events.plainEnglish: The Property Tax Administrator delivers Reports and Opinions for each county regarding level of value and quality of assessment.
events.whyItMatters: Reports and Opinions feed the statewide equalization review before valuation notices and protests.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes extension timing may change this date.

events.title: Reports and Opinions Provided for Larger Counties
events.plainEnglish: For counties at or above the population threshold, the Property Tax Administrator provides Reports and Opinions to the Tax Equalization and Review Commission.
events.whyItMatters: This keeps large-county state equalization timing aligned with the later abstract deadline.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Population-limited timing; verify county applicability.

events.title: Homestead Schedule I Income Tax Filing Checkpoint
events.plainEnglish: Homestead exemption applicants using Schedule I should be aware of the timely income tax return filing date.
events.whyItMatters: Income documentation can affect homestead exemption processing.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes IRS extensions may apply.

events.title: IAAO 102 Fundamentals Workshop
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists IAAO 102 Fundamentals of Real Property Appraisal as an education opportunity.
events.whyItMatters: Education reminders help the admin view function as a working monthly reference.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder lists this as a 33-hour credit in-person workshop.

events.title: County Assessor Coffee Talk
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a county assessor Coffee Talk session.
events.whyItMatters: This gives admin users a quick monthly reminder for training and discussion opportunities.
events.sourceLabel: April 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes no credits are offered.

events.title: Personal Property Return Deadline
events.plainEnglish: Individuals with taxable tangible personal property file the Nebraska Personal Property Return and Schedule with the county assessor by this date.
events.whyItMatters: Missing the deadline can affect penalties for personal property reporting.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; legal citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Keep residential real-property users oriented: this applies to taxable tangible personal property, not ordinary house contents.

events.title: Prior-Year Taxes Delinquent in Smaller Counties
events.plainEnglish: In counties under the stated population threshold, the first half of prior-year real and personal property taxes becomes delinquent if unpaid.
events.whyItMatters: This is about payment status for the prior tax year, not the current assessment value.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; legal citation pending verification
events.adminNote: County population rules affect the delinquency schedule; direct payment questions to the treasurer.

events.title: State Equalization Adjustment Deadline
events.plainEnglish: The state equalization process reaches a deadline for class or subclass valuation adjustments.
events.whyItMatters: This is part of the statewide fairness check before local valuation notices and protests.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; legal citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Track any Tax Equalization and Review Commission action that affects class or subclass valuation.

events.title: Amended Homestead Summary Certificate
events.plainEnglish: County officials file amended homestead exemption summary information when prior-year tax loss needs correction.
events.whyItMatters: This keeps homestead reimbursement records aligned with official exemption activity.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; legal citation pending verification
events.adminNote: PAD notes Form 458X for 2021 and forward is filed electronically through the homestead database.

events.title: Nebraska Advantage and ImagiNE Personal Property Exemption Claims
events.plainEnglish: Taxpayers with signed agreements file Nebraska Advantage Act or ImagiNE Nebraska Act personal property exemption claim materials with the Tax Commissioner and affected county assessors.
events.whyItMatters: This specialized filing can affect exempt personal property records in counties where the project is located.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder references Forms 312P and 1107P.

events.title: Governmental Subdivision Protest Decisions
events.plainEnglish: The County Board of Equalization makes decisions on protests about governmental subdivision property not used for a public purpose.
events.whyItMatters: This completes the public-use property protest path started by assessor notices and April protests.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Connects to March and April governmental subdivision reminders.

events.title: School Adjusted Valuation Correction Requests
events.plainEnglish: A school system or county official may request corrections to school adjusted valuation due to prior-year tax list corrections.
events.whyItMatters: This is a carryover correction point that can affect school adjusted valuation records.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder; statutory citation shown in source PDF
events.adminNote: Admin-only reminder from the monthly assessor calendar.

events.title: Legislative Updates Webinar
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a Legislative Updates webinar for assessor education credit.
events.whyItMatters: Education reminders help the admin view function as a working monthly reference.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder lists this as a 1-credit-hour opportunity.

events.title: Damaged Real Property Webinar
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a Damaged Real Property webinar for assessor education credit.
events.whyItMatters: This aligns training with the damaged-property reporting process that appears later in the year.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder lists this as a 1-credit-hour opportunity.

events.title: County Assessor Coffee Talk
events.plainEnglish: PAD lists a county assessor Coffee Talk session.
events.whyItMatters: This gives admin users a quick monthly reminder for training and discussion opportunities.
events.sourceLabel: May 2026 PAD Monthly Assessor Reminder
events.adminNote: Monthly reminder notes no registration; no credits are offered.

events.title: Valuation Notice Timing
events.plainEnglish: Valuation-change notices are mailed around this point when a property value increased or decreased.
events.whyItMatters: A notice helps taxpayers know whether they should review the record before the protest deadline.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: The main calendar ties this date to assessment-roll completion, published notice, and valuation-change notice mailing.

events.title: Property Valuation Protest Window
events.plainEnglish: Taxpayers may file a property valuation protest with the County Board of Equalization during this period.
events.whyItMatters: This is the main annual window to ask for review of a real property valuation.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Coordinate Form 422 intake, evidence packets, hearing notices, and board schedules.

events.title: County Board of Equalization Protest Review
events.plainEnglish: The County Board of Equalization reviews property valuation protests and makes decisions after protests are filed.
events.whyItMatters: This is when the formal local review process moves from filing to hearings and decisions.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: The main calendar notes possible extended-county review timing; verify county-specific action before publishing.

events.title: Destroyed or Damaged Property Eligibility Date
events.plainEnglish: Significant damage occurring by this point may allow a property owner to report damaged real property for current-year review.
events.whyItMatters: Damage timing can affect whether a reassessment request is available for the current year.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Pair with the July damaged real property report deadline.

events.title: Destroyed or Damaged Property Report Deadline
events.plainEnglish: Owners of real property that suffered significant damage file the damaged real property report with the county clerk by this date.
events.whyItMatters: This separate process can matter when a property was significantly damaged before July.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Track Form 425 filings and assessor reports to the County Board of Equalization.

events.title: Homestead Late Filing Extension Deadline
events.plainEnglish: Some late homestead exemption filings may be considered with County Board approval.
events.whyItMatters: This is a limited safety valve, not a replacement for filing during the regular window.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Use cautious language and direct taxpayers to official eligibility and late-filing instructions.

events.title: Taxable Valuations Certified to Political Subdivisions
events.plainEnglish: County assessors certify taxable valuations to political subdivisions before budgets and levies are finalized.
events.whyItMatters: These certified values become the base used by taxing bodies when setting budgets and levies.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Includes taxable valuations, growth value where applicable, and TIF current valuations.

events.title: Appeal Deadline After County Board Action
events.plainEnglish: A taxpayer may have a deadline to appeal final County Board of Equalization action to the Tax Equalization and Review Commission.
events.whyItMatters: This is a later review path after the county board has acted; deadlines can depend on the county and action taken.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: The main calendar notes an extended-county deadline; verify county-specific appeal timing before public release.

events.title: Budget and Joint Public Hearing Setup
events.plainEnglish: Taxing bodies move from certified values into budget requests, public hearing information, and final budget decisions.
events.whyItMatters: This is where the value base begins turning into the levy and tax-bill side of the year.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Track participating political subdivisions, hearing notices, tax requests, and budget filings.

events.title: Property Tax Credit Certification
events.plainEnglish: State property tax credit amounts are certified for use later in the tax-bill process.
events.whyItMatters: Credits are one of the reasons final tax bills are not just assessed value multiplied by a local rate.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Admin users may need this for reconciling credit data with treasurer and tax-list workflows.

events.title: Levy Setting and Tax Request Actions
events.plainEnglish: After budgets are adopted, taxing bodies and county officials complete levy and tax request actions.
events.whyItMatters: This is where the final tax rate side of the bill comes together after assessment values are already set.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Verify final 2026 budget, levy, and county-specific deadlines before production use.

events.title: Tax List Delivered to County Treasurer
events.plainEnglish: The tax list is completed and delivered to the county treasurer for tax collection work.
events.whyItMatters: This is a major handoff from value and levy work into final tax bills and collection.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Used as a process milestone; taxpayer bill timing should still defer to official county treasurer communications.

events.title: Certificate of Taxes Levied Report Filed
events.plainEnglish: County assessors file the Certificate of Taxes Levied report with the Property Tax Administrator.
events.whyItMatters: The CTL report is a key public record for understanding certified values, taxes levied, and tax-rate patterns.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Connects to the prototype's CTL context and countywide levy history.

events.title: Real and Personal Property Taxes Due
events.plainEnglish: Real and personal property taxes are due at year-end, with later delinquency dates depending on county rules.
events.whyItMatters: Tax bills follow the assessment, equalization, budget, levy, credit, and tax-list steps that happened earlier in the year.
events.sourceLabel: 2025 Nebraska PAD Main Calendar; 2026 main-calendar citation pending verification
events.adminNote: Payment-specific questions belong with the county treasurer and official tax statement.
